title_en: Introduction to Kitab al-Umm and Imam ash-Shafi'i
content_en:
Kitab al-Umm (The Mother of Books) is the foundational legal compendium of Imam Muhammad ibn Idris ash-Shafi'i (150–204 AH / 767–820 CE), the founder of the Shafi'i legal school and the father of Islamic legal theory (usul al-fiqh). The work represents the mature Egyptian-period positions of ash-Shafi'i (al-madhhab al-jadid) — the legal opinions he developed and revised during the final years of his life in Egypt, which superseded his earlier Iraqi-period views (al-madhhab al-qadim) on a number of questions.

Ash-Shafi'i was born in Gaza (according to the most reliable account) and grew up in Makkah, where he memorized the Quran and studied the Arabic poetry of the Hudhayl tribe — acquiring the linguistic mastery that later gave him unparalleled authority in interpreting Quranic and hadith texts. He studied jurisprudence under Malik ibn Anas in Madinah, absorbing the Maliki tradition before developing his own independent legal methodology. He also studied under Hanafi scholars in Iraq, gaining familiarity with the systematic rationalism of the Iraqi tradition. From this comprehensive background, he developed the first systematic theory of Islamic legal sources in his Risalah.

Kitab al-Umm is the product of ash-Shafi'i's teaching sessions in Egypt, compiled by his students — primarily al-Rabi' ibn Sulayman al-Muradi — from his dictation and their notes. It covers the full range of Islamic jurisprudence and includes not only ash-Shafi'i's legal positions but his arguments against the opinions of Malik, Abu Hanifah, and other scholars whose positions he found insufficiently grounded in authenticated hadith. The polemical sections are among the most intellectually stimulating parts of the work.

Ash-Shafi'i's methodology — requiring that all legal rulings be derivable from the Quran, the authenticated Sunnah, the consensus of the scholars (ijma'), and analogy (qiyas) based on explicit textual rationales — represented a paradigm shift in Islamic legal thinking. Before the Risalah, the sources of Islamic law were widely acknowledged but not systematically theorized. After ash-Shafi'i, every subsequent legal school had to either adopt his framework or argue against it — a testament to his intellectual dominance.

Kitab al-Umm is not a commentary on an earlier text (as most later fiqh manuals are) but an original composition directly presenting ash-Shafi'i's legal views with their evidential basis. This makes it a primary source of the first order — what ash-Shafi'i himself believed, argued, and taught. Reading Kitab al-Umm alongside the Risalah gives the student access to Islamic jurisprudence at its formative moment: the period when the discipline of fiqh was taking the systematic shape it would retain for a thousand years.

The work was later abridged by al-Muzani (d. 264 AH), ash-Shafi'i's most distinguished student, in the Mukhtasar al-Muzani — a text that became the basis for the major Shafi'i commentaries including al-Mawardi's Al-Hawi al-Kabir. The relationship between Kitab al-Umm, the Mukhtasar, and the later commentaries represents the standard structure of classical Islamic legal development.
title_id: Pengantar Kitab al-Umm dan Imam asy-Syafi'i
content_id:
Kitab al-Umm (Kitab Induk) adalah kompendium hukum fondasi dari Imam Muhammad ibn Idris asy-Syafi'i (150–204 H / 767–820 M), pendiri madzhab hukum Syafi'i dan bapak ilmu ushul fikih Islam. Ini adalah salah satu teks paling penting dalam sejarah yurisprudensi Islam — sebuah catatan langsung dari pemikiran hukum asy-Syafi'i sendiri pada fase pematangannya, yang membuat posisi-posisinya tersedia bagi generasi-generasi berikutnya dalam kata-katanya sendiri daripada melalui laporan-laporan perantara.

Asy-Syafi'i adalah murid dari Imam Malik di Madinah dan Imam Muhammad ibn al-Hasan asy-Syaibani di Baghdad, pengalaman yang memberinya pengetahuan mendalam tentang tradisi Maliki Hijaz dan tradisi Hanafi Irak. Dari keterlibatannya dengan kedua sekolah, beliau mengembangkan kritiknya terhadap terlalu mengandalkan akal atau tradisi lokal (ra'y) dan bersikeras pada primasi Quran dan Sunnah Nabi ﷺ sebagai sumber-sumber hukum, dengan analogi (qiyas) digunakan hanya ketika sumber-sumber wahyu tidak secara langsung menangani kasus yang ada.

Kitab al-Umm merepresentasikan pemikiran beliau pada fase Mesir karir beliau, yang disebut sebagai qaul jadid (pandangan baru) untuk membedakannya dari pandangan-pandangan sebelumnya yang dikembangkan di Iraq dan Mekah. Fase Mesir ini adalah saat di mana Asy-Syafi'i memiliki akses ke tradisi-tradisi dari berbagai pusat keilmuan Islam dan ketika yurisprudensinya mencapai bentuk kematangannya. Teks Kitab al-Umm sebagaimana kita miliki sekarang disusun oleh muridnya ar-Rabi ibn Sulaiman al-Muradi.
content_ar:
كتاب الأم للإمام محمد بن إدريس الشافعي (ت 204 هـ) يُعدّ من أعظم المصنّفات الفقهية في تاريخ الإسلام، وهو الموسوعة الفقهية الشاملة التي سطّر فيها الإمام الشافعي مذهبه الجديد بعد انتقاله إلى مصر.

ويمتاز الإمام الشافعي بأنه واضع علم أصول الفقه من خلال رسالته الشهيرة، ومؤسس المذهب الشافعي الذي انتشر في مصر وبلاد الشام واليمن وجنوب شرق آسيا. وكتاب الأم هو التطبيق العملي لأصوله ومنهجه الاستنباطي.

يشتمل الكتاب على فقه شامل في العبادات والمعاملات والأحوال الشخصية والجنايات، مع توثيق الخلاف مع المذاهب الأخرى لا سيما مذهب أبي حنيفة ومذهب مالك. وتتميز نصوص الشافعي في هذا الكتاب بالدقة العلمية والربط المتقن بالأدلة.
